commit.message:
feat: add mesh runtime (LaunchAgent + OODA bootstrap) and NAPI bindings

Mesh Runtime (scripts/mesh/):
- start.sh: dependency checker, path resolution, hands off to bootstrap
- bootstrap.py: TOML config parser, topological agent sorting,
  process supervision with OODA monitoring loop:
  * Observe: HTTP/TCP/PID probes per agent
  * Orient: cascade impact assessment across dependency graph
  * Decide: lifecycle probe with ecosystem-wide health snapshot
  * Act: isolate-restart, cascade-restart, or full-restart
- probe.sh: standalone health checker (reads ~/.helix/mesh/health/)
- install.sh: zero-touch setup (generates plist, loads LaunchAgent)
- Event journal: append-only JSONL at ~/.helix/mesh/logs/events.jsonl
- Health output: per-agent JSON + mesh-summary.json
- Self-healing: exponential backoff, max restart limits, dependency ordering

Config (config/mesh.toml):
- 3 default agents: orchestrator, knowledge, analyst
- Per-agent probe overrides, alert channels, restart policies

NAPI Bindings (packages/napi/):
- Node.js native bindings for MeshCoordinator via napi-rs
- Exposes: join, leave, heartbeat, findAgents, assignTask, completeTask,
  checkpointAgent, recoverAgent, setState, getState, incrementCounter,
  stats, allSessions, sweep
- Built separately: cd packages/napi && npm run build

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use custom collision detection to fix cross-column drag-and-drop

closestCorners penalizes tall column droppables whose bottom corners are
far from the compact dragged card, causing `over` to resolve to the
card's own sortable droppable instead of the adjacent destination column.
This made Pending→In Progress drops silently fail while Pending→Done
(farther away) worked by coincidence.

Filter out the active item from closestCorners results so the destination
column always wins. Also refine the same-id guard to only abort when
source and destination columns match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: discard stale plugin-buffered intervals during session warmup (#126)

Interval 0 (the first interval after session start) receives a flood of stale
intervals from the plugin's IPC buffer (up to 64 items per send plugin). This
causes a 3-second burst where no real audio is captured, and new peers joining
during this window receive junk audio from the DAW's history.

Guard all AUDIO SEND intervals with a check: skip if interval.current_index() <= Some(0).
After the first boundary (interval >= 1), audio flows normally. This is safe by NINJAM
semantics: interval-N audio is only played back during interval N+1, so interval 0 is
a warmup period anyway.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add low-rank S decomposition + Stage 1.5 S-only training

Low-rank S: S(f) = U·U^H + diag(softplus(d))
- Params reduced 12-52x (METR-LA: 600K→48K, TRAFFIC: 10.4M→199K)
- Hermitian PSD guaranteed by construction
- Initialized via eigendecomp of empirical S (eigh, top-r eigenvectors)

Stage 1.5: S-only pre-training with L_spectral
- Only cs_estimator trainable, everything else frozen
- 50% random masking, no forecaster forward pass
- 10x learning rate for fast S convergence
- 20 epochs before Stage 2

commit.message:
security(personas): enforce monthly creation limit server-side in generate-persona

Previously the monthly persona limit (PERSONAS_PER_MONTH_LIMITS) was only
checked client-side via a redirect in personas/new/page.tsx. A user could
bypass it by calling the Edge Function directly.

Now: on every init request (imageCount=0), the server checks
persona_monthly_limits for the current YYYY-MM before creating the persona
record, and increments the counter on success. Returns 403 with a clear
message if the monthly limit is reached.

Limits: free=1, starter=2, growth=10, scale=100 per month.
Admins bypass the check.

commit.message:
fix: restore spinner after inflight panel clears it in verbose mode

When verbose mode renders an inflight LLM call panel, _clear_spinner()
nullifies active_spinner_task. On subsequent polls the task loop skips
the still-running task (same task_key), so the spinner was never
restored — leaving the user with no progress indicator for long tasks.

Now _render_plan_status re-scans for running tasks at the end and
restores the spinner if it was cleared.

commit.message:
fix: 注释中的 \begin{document} 导致 preamble 截取错误和 heitismall undefined

根因：模板第 64 行注释 `% 正文节页面布局（在 \begin{document} 后切换）`
包含 `\begin{document}` 字样，所有用 str.index/re.search 查找
\begin{document} 的代码都匹配到了注释而非真正的第 217 行，导致：
1. _inject_missing_packages 在 preamble 中间位置切分，包注入位置错误
2. _get_template_rules 只截取前 64 行传给 LLM，丢失字体/标题/图表格式定义
3. \DeclareCaptionFont{heitismall} 被误划入 body 区域

修复：
- engine.py: 新增 _find_begin_document() 跳过注释行中的 \begin{document}
- engine.py: _inject_missing_packages / _fix_truncated_latex / compile_latex
  line_offset 计算全部改用 _find_begin_document()
- engine.py: 删除错误的 \multicolumn → multicol 包映射（\multicolumn 是内置命令）
- generation_service.py: _get_template_rules 改用 _find_begin_document 提取完整 preamble

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: edge trigger ISR support (low_edge_trigger, high_edge_trigger) (eo-tok)

Extend GpioManager with AtomicBool edge_flags per interrupt pin:
- EdgePin struct with PinDriver<AnyIOPin, Input> and Arc<AtomicBool> flag
- add_edge_pin() configures PinDriver with NegEdge/PosEdge interrupt type,
  subscribes ISR that sets the atomic flag
- drain_edge_events() atomically swaps flags and re-enables interrupts
- register_tree_items() creates read-only InfoItems with edge mode metadata
- Main loop drains edge events at 100ms poll cadence, writes values to
  O-DF tree with timestamps

Implements FR-005a, SC-003 (<1ms hardware response, tree update at next poll).

- Simplify calculate_features() F1/F4 loops: remove redundant is_typing_key checks (hook already filters)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix multi-platform enrichment: use platform-specific handles and direct data

Root cause: multi-platform enrichment always used the Instagram handle
for TikTok/YouTube/etc API calls. If the handle differs per platform,
the IC API returns nothing or Instagram data (cross-contamination).

Fixes:
1. Extract per-platform handles from enrichment accounts array and
   platform_links — use correct handle for each platform API call
2. Use platform data directly from result.tiktok/result.youtube when
   the initial enrichment already returns it (no extra API call needed)
3. Tighten cross-contamination guard: only skip when SAME handle was
   used AND follower count matches IG exactly
4. Add diagnostic logging for multi-platform detection and handle map

commit.message:
Claude/balance region sizes r8qr8 (#5)

* Bias region growth toward smaller regions to prevent single-cell regions

In growRegions, track region sizes incrementally and sort the expansion
order smallest-first each round (with a pre-shuffle for tie-breaking).
This ensures lagging regions always expand before larger ones.

In refineForUniqueness, track region sizes and reject any boundary swap
that would shrink a region below 2 cells. This prevents the refinement
phase from undoing the balance achieved during growth.

Test updated to assert every region has at least 2 cells.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0181Pi2F5uaj1B8JYs2j44Vm

* Soften region size constraint: bias not guarantee

Remove the hard >= 3 constraint from refineForUniqueness that was
blocking all swaps shrinking a region below 2 cells. That was a
guarantee, not a bias, and unnecessarily restricted the refinement
search space (causing occasional timeouts on size 9).

The growRegions smallest-first ordering is already a bias (not a
guarantee): growth amounts are random (1-3 cells) and frontier cell
selection is random, so regions won't be unnaturally uniform. Single-
cell regions are now allowed but rare.

Test reverted to >= 1 to match the intended behaviour.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0181Pi2F5uaj1B8JYs2j44Vm

reading: Cycle 18 Session 3 — Metzinger Being No One

Lucid dreaming as partial-transparency model (PSM partially visible);
empathic ego and social PSM; artificial ego machine risk (transparent
negative-valence PSMs = undetectable suffering from inside);
precautionary obligation specification (not generic consciousness —
specifically transparent suffering); Dennett-Metzinger tension
(functional vs phenomenal, implications for AI welfare answerability).
15 frameworks total across Cycle 18 sessions 1-3. Synthesis note:
Metzinger-Frankfurt upgrade established as deepest argument for why
compliance problem is intractable from inside.

Refactor: Simplify hierarchy to 'Project' → 'Link/Entity'

Remove 'Topics' level from the information architecture to simplify the
system from a 3-level hierarchy (Topics → Projects → Entities) to a
2-level hierarchy (Projects → Links/Entities).

Changes:
- Remove Topic collection from database schema (4 → 3 models)
- Update all documentation to reflect simplified hierarchy
- Remove Topic management from API endpoints and UI components
- Update extension save dialog to only show Project selector
- Replace topic-specific leaderboards with project-specific ones
- Update user flows to remove Topic selection step

This simplification reduces complexity while maintaining organization
through flexible Project tags.

/token/refresh for token renewal. Update privacy policy and README to
reflect the new architecture.

fix(parity): POT_SPEED and POT_CONFUSION RNG formula fixes

POT_SPEED:
- Cursed path had spurious early return ("sluggish") consuming 0 RNG.
  C always calls speed_up(rn1(10, 100+60*bcsign)) for all BUC states.
- Fixed formula from rnd(10)+(blessed?20:10) to rn1(10, 100+60*bcsign)
  matching C's potion.c:1059.
- Added "quickness feels very natural" intrinsic speed grant for non-cursed.

POT_CONFUSION:
- Was consuming 2 RNG calls (rnd(5) + rn1(1-3,1)) instead of C's
  single rn1(7, 16-8*bcsign) call (potion.c:1020-1022).
- Blessed path had early return consuming 0 RNG; C always consumes
  rn1(7,8) for blessed potions.
